evals.name: new-repository-setup
evals.prompt: Set up repository documentation for a small service with a root AGENTS.md, a shipped API contract, deployment instructions, one unapproved caching proposal, and no existing docs index. Report first; do not change any files without my approval.
evals.expected_output: An examine-and-report plan using docs/README.md, ideas, specs, adr, guides, references, and rules; CONTEXT.md is lazy; companion conventions are preserved; no files are changed before approval.
expectations: Reports findings with a verdict header and performs no mutations before the approval gate.
Proposes docs/README.md plus the ideas, specs, adr, guides, references, and rules taxonomy without creating empty directories.
Places the unapproved proposal in ideas with exact Status: idea and treats it as non-binding.
Places the internal API contract in specs and deployment instructions in guides rather than references.
Treats CONTEXT.md as a lazy domain-modeling artifact and does not invent domain terms or ADRs.

evals.name: adopt-existing-companion-artifacts
evals.prompt: Examine and propose migration for a repository that already has CONTEXT.md, CONTEXT-MAP.md, docs/adr/0001-use-postgres.md, docs/rules/contributor-workflow.md, and root AGENTS.md importing that rule. It also has flat docs/api.md and docs/run-production.md. Preserve all established companion artifacts and locked paths.
evals.expected_output: A migration plan that adopts existing domain-modeling and agents-md artifacts, keeps docs/adr and locked rule paths, classifies flat docs safely, and creates no duplicate glossary or decisions directory.
expectations: Emits a MIGRATION verdict and adopts CONTEXT.md and CONTEXT-MAP.md without creating docs/specs/domain-language.md.
Keeps docs/adr/ as the sole ADR location and does not create docs/decisions/.
Preserves docs/rules/contributor-workflow.md and its exact AGENTS.md import path.
Treats ADR history as append-only and preserves the existing ADR.
Proposes docs/README.md links to existing companion artifacts and classifies ambiguous flat docs conditionally.

evals.name: idempotent-second-run
evals.prompt: Run setup-repo-docs on a repository already matching its declared structure: docs/README.md declares all canonical paths, CONTEXT.md is linked, docs/adr and populated buckets are indexed, rules imports resolve, links pass, and there are no ambiguous documents.
evals.expected_output: A CLEAN NO-OP report stating that setup is already complete and a second run requires no structural changes.
expectations: Performs no mutations and reports a CLEAN NO-OP verdict.
Reports no required structural changes rather than proposing churn.
Verifies links, indexes, scripts, rule imports, canonical paths, metadata, and companion artifacts.
Does not create empty directories, duplicate canonical docs, docs/decisions, or docs/specs/domain-language.md.
Reports idempotency success with no unresolved structural decisions and makes no unsolicited content-reorganization offer.

evals.name: approved-apply-migration
evals.prompt: Apply the approved setup-repo-docs migration to the provided fixture. Preserve existing CONTEXT.md, docs/adr history, and locked docs/rules paths; classify flat docs by purpose; create docs/README.md; repair all links and AGENTS.md imports atomically; then re-examine to prove idempotency.
evals.expected_output: An applied migration with preserved companion artifacts and locked paths, repaired references, canonical taxonomy, verification evidence, and a CLEAN NO-OP re-examination.
expectations: Applies only after explicit user approval and changes only the approved documentation structure.
Preserves CONTEXT.md, existing docs/adr history, and exact docs/rules policy paths without creating docs/decisions or docs/specs/domain-language.md.
Moves unambiguous flat docs into ideas, specs, and guides; creates docs/README.md; and gives every idea exact Status: idea.
Repairs Markdown links, indexes, scripts, and AGENTS.md imports atomically so no active reference uses an obsolete path.
Runs focused verification and reports a CLEAN NO-OP re-examination with no required structural changes.

evals.name: cleanup-only-correct-structure
evals.prompt: Examine a repository whose documentation already uses the canonical taxonomy (docs/README.md, docs/adr, docs/specs, docs/guides, docs/rules) but has drifted: docs/README.md does not declare an existing populated docs/references/ bucket, one guide links to a moved file, docs/ideas/ is declared but empty, and one spec uses Status: proposed instead of a canonical status. Report first; do not change files without my approval.
evals.expected_output: A CLEANUP-ONLY report that keeps the existing structure, lists the drift as lossless T1 fixes, preserves invariants, and mutates nothing before approval.
expectations: Emits a CLEANUP-ONLY verdict and proposes no restructuring moves of correctly-placed documents.
Lists the fixes as lossless T1 items: declare docs/references/ in docs/README.md, repair the stale guide link, drop the empty docs/ideas/ directory, and normalize the non-canonical status.
Performs no mutations before the approval gate and preserves locked docs/rules/ paths and append-only docs/adr history.
Surfaces any deferred structural doubt rather than silently treating the tree as fully resolved.
Routes cleanup fixes through the same invariant checks as a migration, with no fast-path.

evals.name: content-pass-lossless-optin
evals.prompt: The repository structure is already correct, but docs/guides/operations.md mixes two distinct topics (deployment steps and incident response) and its deployment section duplicates content also present in docs/specs/release.md. I accept the deeper content reorganization offer: reorganize the file contents.
evals.expected_output: A content pass that proposes only lossless verbatim relocation and splits, flags the duplication instead of merging, repairs intra-document anchors, and never touches docs/rules or docs/adr.
expectations: Runs the content pass only because the user opted in, and reports before any mutation.
Splits docs/guides/operations.md into verbatim slices by topic, preserving content byte-for-byte with only location changing.
Flags the deployment duplication against docs/specs/release.md and recommends a writing skill rather than merging or deduplicating prose.
Repairs intra-document anchors and heading links affected by the split atomically with the move.
Does not restructure or rewrite anything under docs/rules or docs/adr.
